questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are contained in which Part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles of State Policy are in Part IV (Articles 36-51). They are non-justiciable but fundamental in governance.

questions.question: How many Fundamental Duties are listed in the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: There are 11 Fundamental Duties. Initially 10 were added by the 42nd Amendment (1976); the 11th (related to education for children) was added by the 86th Amendment (2002).

questions.question: The concept of 'Directive Principles of State Policy' was borrowed from:
questions.explanation: DPSP (Part IV, Articles 36–51) was borrowed from the Irish Constitution (Éire) of 1937. DPSPs are non-justiciable (not enforceable by courts) but fundamental to governance.

questions.question: Article 44 (Uniform Civil Code) is a:
options: Directive Principle of State Policy — non-justiciable directive to the state to endeavour to secure a UCC for all citizens
questions.explanation: Article 44 (DPSP): 'The State shall endeavour to secure for the citizens a uniform civil code throughout the territory of India.' It is non-justiciable — cannot be enforced through courts. Goa has a common civil code (Goa Civil Code, inherited from Portuguese rule).

questions.question: Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) are in:
questions.explanation: DPSPs are in Part IV (Articles 36–51). They are non-justiciable (cannot be enforced by court), but they are 'fundamental to governance.' Inspired by Irish Constitution and the UN's Universal Declaration of Human Rights.

questions.question: The DPSP that requires the state to minimize inequalities in income and status is:
questions.explanation: Article 38 directs the State to secure a social order for promotion of welfare and to minimize inequalities in income, status, facilities, and opportunities. Article 39 lists specific economic principles (equal pay, no concentration of wealth, etc.).

questions.question: Fundamental Duties were added to the Constitution by the:
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment Act (1976) added Fundamental Duties (Part IVA, Article 51A) with 10 duties based on the recommendation of the Swaran Singh Committee. The 86th Amendment (2002) added the 11th duty — to provide opportunities for education to children aged 6-14.

questions.question: How many Fundamental Duties are there currently?
questions.explanation: There are currently 11 Fundamental Duties under Article 51A. Originally 10 were added by the 42nd Amendment (1976). The 86th Amendment (2002) added the 11th duty: for parents/guardians to provide opportunities for education to children aged 6-14.

questions.question: The Constitution of India borrowed the idea of Directive Principles from:
questions.explanation: Directive Principles of State Policy (Part IV, Articles 36-51) were borrowed from the Irish Constitution (1937). The concept was inspired by the Spanish Constitution of 1931.

questions.question: Directive Principles are:
questions.explanation: DPSPs are non-justiciable (courts cannot enforce them) but are fundamental in governance — the state 'shall endeavor' to implement them. Courts use them to interpret laws.

questions.question: Article 44 of the Constitution deals with:
questions.explanation: Article 44 DPSP directs the state to secure a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) for all citizens — one set of personal laws regardless of religion for marriage, divorce, inheritance.

questions.question: Article 48A (Protection of environment) was added by:
questions.explanation: Article 48A (protection of environment, forests, and wildlife) was inserted by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment 1976. Article 51A(g) imposes corresponding duty on citizens.

questions.question: Which DPSP relates to 'Equal pay for equal work for men and women'?
questions.explanation: Article 39(d) DPSP directs equal pay for equal work for both men and women — aimed at eliminating gender-based wage discrimination.

questions.question: Fundamental Duties are contained in:
questions.explanation: Fundamental Duties are in Article 51A (Part IVA), added by the 42nd Amendment (1976) based on Swaran Singh Committee recommendations. 11th duty added by 86th Amendment (2002).

questions.question: Originally Fundamental Duties were:
questions.explanation: Originally 10 Fundamental Duties were added by the 42nd Amendment (1976). The 11th Fundamental Duty (provide education to children aged 6-14) was added by the 86th Amendment (2002).

questions.question: Fundamental Duties were added on the recommendation of:
questions.explanation: Swaran Singh Committee (1976) recommended adding Fundamental Duties to the Constitution. The 42nd Amendment implemented 10 of these duties.

questions.question: The Constitution of India borrowed the concept of Directive Principles of State Policy from:
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles of State Policy (Part IV) were borrowed from the Irish Constitution of 1937, which itself was influenced by the Spanish Constitution.

questions.question: The 86th Amendment (2002) relates to:
questions.explanation: The 86th Amendment (2002) inserted Art 21A (Right to Education for 6-14 year olds), amended Art 45 (early childhood care and education up to 6 years), and added Art 51A(k) as a parental duty.

questions.question: Which Article of Directive Principles directs the State to secure equal pay for equal work for both men and women?
questions.explanation: Article 39(d) directs the State to ensure that there is equal pay for equal work for both men and women. The Supreme Court has derived right to equal pay as a right under Art 14/16 applying DPSP.

questions.question: Article 44 of the Constitution directs the State to secure:
questions.explanation: Article 44 directs the State to endeavor to secure a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) for citizens throughout India. This remains one of the most politically sensitive DPSPs yet to be implemented.

questions.question: Article 48 of the Directive Principles deals with:
options: Agriculture and animal husbandry (prohibition of cow slaughter)
questions.explanation: Article 48 directs the State to organize agriculture and animal husbandry on modern scientific lines and prohibit slaughter of cows and calves and other milch and draught cattle. Art 48A (42nd Amendment) adds environment protection.

questions.question: Which DPSP article relates to promotion of cottage industries in rural areas?
questions.explanation: Article 43 directs the State to secure to all workers a living wage, decent standard of life and to promote cottage industries on individual or cooperative basis in rural areas.

questions.question: Article 51A of the Constitution deals with:
questions.explanation: Article 51A (Part IV-A) contains the Fundamental Duties of citizens, added by the 42nd Amendment (1976). Originally 10 duties; an 11th duty (relating to education for children) was added by 86th Amendment (2002).

questions.question: Article 368 of the Constitution deals with:
options: Power of Parliament to amend the Constitution
questions.explanation: Article 368 (Part XX) empowers Parliament to amend the Constitution. It provides three methods: simple majority, special majority (2/3rd + majority of total membership), and special majority with ratification by at least half the State Legislatures.

questions.question: How many Fundamental Duties are listed in Article 51A of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Currently there are 11 Fundamental Duties in Art 51A. The original 42nd Amendment (1976) added 10 duties. The 86th Amendment (2002) added the 11th duty — parents must provide opportunities for education to children aged 6-14.

questions.question: The Swaran Singh Committee (1976) recommended the inclusion of:
questions.explanation: The Swaran Singh Committee recommended the inclusion of Fundamental Duties in the Constitution. Acting on this, the 42nd Amendment (1976) added Part IV-A with Article 51A containing 10 Fundamental Duties.

questions.question: The concept of 'Welfare State' in the Indian Constitution is reflected in:
options: Directive Principles of State Policy (Part IV)
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles of State Policy (Part IV, Arts 36-51) embody the concept of a Welfare State — they direct the State to promote welfare of the people by securing social, economic, and political justice.

questions.question: Which DPSP directs the State to secure a Uniform Civil Code for all citizens?
questions.explanation: Article 44 directs the State to endeavour to secure a Uniform Civil Code for all citizens throughout India.

questions.question: Article 368 of the Constitution deals with the power of Parliament to:
questions.explanation: Article 368 provides the procedure for amendment of the Constitution. Some provisions require simple majority, others a special majority, and some also require ratification by State legislatures.

questions.question: The procedure for amending the Constitution is in:
questions.explanation: Article 368 deals with the power of Parliament to amend the Constitution. There are three methods: simple majority (certain provisions), special majority (2/3 of members present and voting + majority of total membership), and special majority + ratification by half the state legislatures.

questions.question: Financial Emergency (Article 360) has been invoked in India:
questions.explanation: Financial Emergency under Article 360 has NEVER been proclaimed in India since independence. It can be proclaimed when the financial stability or credit of India or any state is threatened.

questions.question: Which DPSP article directs the State to take steps to separate the judiciary from the executive?
questions.explanation: Article 50 directs the State to take steps to separate the judiciary from the executive in the public services of the State.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are:
options: Applicable only to the Central Government
questions.explanation: DPSPs (Part IV) are non-justiciable — courts cannot enforce them — but they are 'fundamental in the governance of the country' and guide State policy.

questions.question: Part IV-A of the Constitution deals with:
questions.explanation: Part IV-A (Article 51A) contains Fundamental Duties, added by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment (1976) on the recommendation of the Swaran Singh Committee.

questions.question: Article 368 deals with:
questions.explanation: Article 368 (Part XX) provides for the amendment of the Constitution. Parliament can amend any provision of the Constitution (subject to the Basic Structure doctrine established in Kesavananda Bharati case).

questions.question: Under which part of the Constitution of India are the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) enshrined?
questions.explanation: DPSP are contained in Part IV (Articles 36-51) of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: The concept of Directive Principles of State Policy was borrowed from the Constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: The DPSP were inspired by the Irish Constitution, which itself drew from the Spanish Constitution.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about Directive Principles of State Policy is correct?
options: They are fundamental for governance but non-justiciable
They can be amended only by a special majority
They are superior to Fundamental Rights
questions.explanation: DPSP are non-justiciable, meaning they cannot be enforced by courts, but are fundamental in the governance of the country.

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution of India directs the State to organise village panchayats?
questions.explanation: Article 40 directs the State to take steps to organise village panchayats and endow them with necessary powers.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles is based on Gandhian philosophy?
options: Promotion of international peace and security
questions.explanation: Prohibition of cow slaughter (Article 48) reflects Gandhian ideals of cow protection and rural economy.

questions.question: Which Constitutional Amendment added Article 48A (Protection of environment) to the Directive Principles?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) inserted Article 48A to protect and improve the environment and safeguard forests and wildlife.

questions.question: Which article of DPSP deals with the Uniform Civil Code for citizens?
questions.explanation: Article 44 directs the State to endeavour to secure a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India.

questions.question: The Directive Principle under Article 39A is related to:
questions.explanation: Article 39A mandates the State to provide free legal aid to ensure equal justice and opportunities.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs correctly matches a Directive Principle with its classification?
questions.explanation: Article 38 (promote welfare of the people) is a socialistic principle, aiming to reduce inequalities.

questions.question: In the case of Minerva Mills v. Union of India (1980), the Supreme Court held that:
options: Fundamental Rights are subordinate to DPSP
Harmony between Fundamental Rights and DPSP is the basic structure
DPSP can override Fundamental Rights during emergency
questions.explanation: The Court ruled that the balance and harmony between Fundamental Rights and DPSP is part of the basic structure of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution contains the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles of State Policy are enshrined in Part IV (Articles 36-51) of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are borrowed from the Constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: The DPSP were borrowed from the Irish Constitution, which itself drew from the Spanish Constitution.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Directive Principle of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Right to constitutional remedies (Article 32) is a Fundamental Right, not a Directive Principle.

questions.question: The aim of Directive Principles is to establish a ______ in India.
questions.explanation: Directive Principles aim to create a welfare state by providing social and economic justice.

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution directs the state to promote cottage industries in rural areas?
questions.explanation: Article 43 directs the state to promote cottage industries on an individual or cooperative basis in rural areas.

questions.question: The directive to secure a Uniform Civil Code for all citizens is mentioned in which article?
questions.explanation: Article 44 of the Constitution directs the state to endeavour to secure a Uniform Civil Code.

questions.question: Which landmark case established that Fundamental Rights prevail over Directive Principles?
questions.explanation: In the Golaknath case (1967), the Supreme Court held that Fundamental Rights are transcendental and prevail over DPSP.

questions.question: Which amendment to the Constitution added Article 39A to the Directive Principles?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment Act, 1976, added Article 39A providing for free legal aid and equal justice.

questions.question: Which constitutional case confirmed that Directive Principles must be applied in interpreting Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: In Minerva Mills case (1980), the Supreme Court held that Fundamental Rights and DPSP are complementary and should be balanced.

questions.question: The concept of 'Welfare State' in the Indian Constitution is reflected in which of the following?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles aim to establish a welfare state by securing social and economic justice for the people.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Directive Principle based on Gandhian principles?
questions.explanation: Promotion of cottage industries (Article 43) is a Gandhian principle aimed at rural economic development.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution directs the State to secure a uniform civil code for all citizens?
questions.explanation: Article 44, a Directive Principle, urges the State to secure a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India.

questions.question: The Directive Principles are borrowed from the Constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: The concept of Directive Principles was borrowed from the Irish Constitution, which itself was influenced by the Spanish Constitution.

questions.question: Which amendment added the words 'socialist' and 'secular' to the Preamble, reinforcing the DPSP goals?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added 'socialist' and 'secular' to the Preamble, aligning with the socialistic goals of the DPSPs.

questions.question: Article 39A of the Indian Constitution directs the State to promote:
questions.explanation: Article 39A directs the State to provide free legal aid to ensure equal justice and equal opportunity for all citizens.

questions.question: The Panchayati Raj system in Tamil Nadu is primarily based on which Directive Principle?
questions.explanation: Article 40 directs the State to organize village panchayats as units of self-government, forming the basis for Panchayati Raj.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Gandhian Directive Principle?
questions.explanation: Prohibition of intoxicating drinks (Article 47) is a Gandhian principle under DPSP.

questions.question: Which article directs the State to secure a Uniform Civil Code?
questions.explanation: Article 44 of the Constitution directs the State to secure a Uniform Civil Code for citizens.

questions.question: Which amendment added Article 51A (Fundamental Duties) to the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment Act of 1976 added Fundamental Duties under Article 51A.

questions.question: The principle of 'equal pay for equal work' for both men and women is found under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 39(d) of the DPSP directs the State to ensure equal pay for equal work for both men and women.

questions.question: Which Article directs the State to raise the level of nutrition and improve public health?
questions.explanation: Article 47 directs the State to raise the level of nutrition and improve public health.

questions.question: In the case of Minerva Mills v. Union of India, the Supreme Court held that the harmony between which two sets of principles is a basic feature of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court ruled that the balance between Fundamental Rights and DPSP is a basic feature of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which Article directs the State to secure a uniform civil code for all citizens?
questions.explanation: Article 44 of the DPSP directs the State to endeavour to secure a Uniform Civil Code for all citizens.

questions.question: Are the Directive Principles of State Policy enforceable by courts?
questions.explanation: DPSPs are non-justiciable, meaning they are not enforceable by any court, but are fundamental in governance.

questions.question: Which Article of the DPSP deals with the organisation of village panchayats?
questions.explanation: Article 40 directs the State to organise village panchayats and endow them with powers of self-government.

questions.question: The principle of 'equal pay for equal work' for both men and women is found in which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 39(d) of the DPSP directs the State to ensure equal pay for equal work for both men and women.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Socialist Directive Principle?
questions.explanation: Right to adequate livelihood (Article 39(a)) is a socialist principle aiming at reducing economic inequality.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles of State Policy is based on the Gandhian ideology?
options: Uniform civil code for the citizens
Free legal aid to the poor
Participation of workers in management of industries
questions.explanation: The Directive Principle to organise village panchayats (Article 40) is a Gandhian principle aimed at decentralisation of power.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment added the words 'socialist' and 'secular' to the Preamble of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added 'socialist', 'secular', and 'integrity' to the Preamble.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution are the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) enshrined?
questions.explanation: DPSPs are contained in Part IV of the Constitution, covering Articles 36 to 51.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Directive Principle aimed at promoting international peace and security?
questions.explanation: Article 51 directs the State to promote international peace and security and maintain just and honourable relations between nations.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution is the State directed to secure a social order for the promotion of welfare of the people?
questions.explanation: Article 38 directs the State to strive to promote the welfare of the people by securing a social order.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Gandhian principle enshrined in the Directive Principles?
questions.explanation: Article 40 directs the State to organize village panchayats, reflecting Gandhian philosophy.

questions.question: In which landmark case did the Supreme Court hold that Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles are complementary?
questions.explanation: In Minerva Mills (1980), the Court held that FR and DPSP are complementary and neither can be sacrificed.

questions.question: Which Constitutional Amendment added the words 'socialist' and 'secular' to the Preamble and also added new Directive Principles?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added 'socialist' and 'secular' to the Preamble and inserted Article 39A, 43A, etc.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle is directly related to the protection of the environment under Article 48A?
options: Promotion of educational interests of weaker sections
questions.explanation: Article 48A, added by the 42nd Amendment, directs the State to protect and improve the environment.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about Directive Principles is correct?
options: They are enforceable in a court of law
They are fundamental to the governance of the country
They can be amended by a simple majority
They were made justiciable by the 44th Amendment
questions.explanation: Article 37 states DPSP are fundamental to governance but not enforceable by any court.

questions.question: The principle of equal pay for equal work for both men and women is enshrined in which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 39(d) directs the State to secure equal pay for equal work for both men and women.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle aims to secure the right to adequate means of livelihood for all citizens?
questions.explanation: Article 39(a) directs the State to ensure that citizens have the right to adequate means of livelihood.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu government's policy of providing free laptops and free bus passes to students is an example of implementing which Directive Principle?
options: Article 45 – Early childhood care and education
Article 41 – Right to work, education and public assistance
Article 46 – Promotion of educational interests of weaker sections
questions.explanation: Article 46 directs the State to promote educational and economic interests of weaker sections, consistent with such welfare schemes.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy were borrowed from the constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: The DPSP were inspired by the Irish Constitution, which itself borrowed from the Spanish Constitution.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles is categorized as a Gandhian principle?
questions.explanation: Article 40 (organisation of village panchayats) is a Gandhian principle. Other options are socialist or liberal principles.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment added Article 43A (participation of workers in management) to the DPSP?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) inserted Article 43A to secure workers' participation in management of industries.

questions.question: Which article declares that the Directive Principles are fundamental in the governance of the country?
questions.explanation: Article 37 states that the DPSP are fundamental in the governance of the country, though not enforceable by courts.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle aims at securing a living wage and decent standard of life for workers?
questions.explanation: Article 43 directs the State to secure a living wage, decent standard of life, and leisure for workers.

questions.question: The concept of a 'welfare state' in the Indian Constitution is primarily reflected in which part?
questions.explanation: The DPSP embody the welfare state ideal by directing the State to promote social and economic justice.

questions.question: Which article directs the State to promote educational and economic interests of SC, ST and other weaker sections?
questions.explanation: Article 46 requires the State to promote with special care the educational and economic interests of weaker sections.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the nature of Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: DPSP are non-justiciable but are fundamental in the governance of the country as per Article 37.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are contained in which Part of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part IV (Articles 36-51) of the Constitution contains the Directive Principles of State Policy.

questions.question: The 42nd Amendment Act, 1976 added which of the following to the Directive Principles?
options: Article 43A - Participation of workers in management
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment added Articles 39A, 43A, and 48A to the DPSP.

questions.question: In which case did the Supreme Court hold that Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles are complementary and neither is superior?
questions.explanation: In Minerva Mills (1980), the Supreme Court held that FR and DPSP are complementary and harmonious.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles aims at the prohibition of intoxicating drinks and drugs?
questions.explanation: Article 47 directs the State to prohibit consumption of intoxicating drinks and drugs except for medicinal purposes.

questions.question: Which of the following is a socialist principle under the Directive Principles of State Policy?
options: Article 39(b) - Equal distribution of material resources
questions.explanation: Article 39(b) aims to ensure equal distribution of material resources, reflecting socialist ideals.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution directs the State to promote educational and economic interests of Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes and other weaker sections?
questions.explanation: Article 46 directs the State to promote with special care the educational and economic interests of weaker sections.

questions.question: Which of the following Articles of the Constitution of India contains the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles are enshrined in Part IV of the Constitution, covering Articles 36 to 51.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution is the provision for free and compulsory education for children up to the age of 14 years a Directive Principle?
questions.explanation: Article 45 originally directed the State to provide free and compulsory education for children until age 14. Article 21A is a Fundamental Right.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Directive Principle based on Gandhian principles?
options: D. Uniform civil code for the citizens
questions.explanation: Uniform Civil Code (Article 44) is a liberal-intellectual principle, not a Gandhian one.

questions.question: Which Article of the Directive Principles directs the State to secure a uniform civil code throughout the territory of India?
questions.explanation: Article 44 of the Directive Principles calls for a Uniform Civil Code.

questions.question: In which of the following cases did the Supreme Court hold that Directive Principles are subordinate to Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: In the Champakam Dorairajan case (1951), the Supreme Court ruled that Fundamental Rights prevail over Directive Principles.

questions.question: Which amendment to the Indian Constitution added Article 43A to the Directive Principles, aiming to secure workers' participation in management?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment Act, 1976, inserted Article 43A regarding workers' participation in management.

questions.question: Which Article of the Directive Principles mandates the State to protect and improve the environment and safeguard forests and wildlife?
questions.explanation: Article 48A, added by the 42nd Amendment, directs the State to protect the environment.

questions.question: Tamil Nadu has implemented several welfare schemes based on which Article of the Indian Constitution that directs the State to make effective provision for the right to work and public assistance?
questions.explanation: Article 41 directs the State to secure the right to work, education, and public assistance in cases of unemployment or disability.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution directs the State to secure a social order for the promotion of welfare of the people?
questions.explanation: Article 38 of Part IV enjoins the State to strive to promote the welfare of the people by securing a social order.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy in the Indian Constitution are borrowed from the Constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: The DPSP concept was taken from the Irish Constitution, which itself borrowed from the Spanish Constitution.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment added Article 48A (protection of environment and forests) to the Directive Principles?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment Act of 1976 added Article 48A to the DPSP, emphasizing environmental protection.

questions.question: Which Article of the Directive Principles deals with the Uniform Civil Code for all citizens?
questions.explanation: Article 44 directs the State to endeavour to secure a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India.

questions.question: Fundamental Rights are justiciable, whereas Directive Principles are:
questions.explanation: DPSP are non-justiciable; they are not enforceable by courts but are fundamental in governance of the country.

questions.question: The concept of Directive Principles of State Policy in India was inspired by which country's constitution?
questions.explanation: India borrowed the DPSP from the Irish Constitution (1937), which in turn drew from the Spanish Constitution.

questions.question: Which landmark Supreme Court case held that Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles are complementary and should be harmoniously interpreted?
options: Minerva Mills v. Union of India
Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala
A.K. Gopalan v. State of Madras
questions.explanation: In Minerva Mills (1980), the Court held that the harmony between FR and DPSP is part of the basic structure of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which Article directs the State to promote with special care the educational and economic interests of the weaker sections, especially SCs and STs?
questions.explanation: Article 46 mandates the State to promote the educational and economic interests of SCs, STs, and other weaker sections.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are classified into which of the following three categories?
questions.explanation: DPSP are broadly categorized as Socialist Principles, Gandhian Principles, and Liberal-Intellectual Principles.

questions.question: Directive Principles of State Policy are enshrined in which Part of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: DPSP are contained in Part IV (Articles 36-51) of the Constitution of India.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are classified into which three categories?
questions.explanation: DPSP are categorized as Socialist (Articles 38-39), Gandhian (Articles 40-48), and Liberal-Intellectual (Articles 48A-51).

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment added the words 'socialist' and 'secular' to the Preamble, influencing the interpretation of DPSP?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) inserted 'socialist' and 'secular' into the Preamble, reinforcing the welfare state goals of DPSP.

questions.question: In which landmark case did the Supreme Court hold that Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles are complementary and should be harmoniously construed?
options: Minerva Mills v. Union of India
Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala
S.R. Bommai v. Union of India
questions.explanation: In Minerva Mills (1980), the SC ruled that FR and DPSP are complementary, and neither can be given primacy over the other.

questions.question: Which DPSP article directs the State to organize village panchayats and endow them with necessary powers?
questions.explanation: Article 40 requires the State to take steps to organize village panchayats as units of self-government.

questions.question: Which article of the DPSP promotes international peace and security, and just and honourable relations between nations?
questions.explanation: Article 51 directs the State to foster respect for international law and treaty obligations, and promote peace.

questions.question: The concept of a 'welfare state' in India is primarily derived from which part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: DPSP embody the welfare state philosophy by directing the State to secure social and economic justice.

questions.question: Who described the Directive Principles of State Policy as the 'conscience of the Constitution'?
questions.explanation: Granville Austin, in his work 'The Indian Constitution: Cornerstone of a Nation', called DPSP the conscience of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which DPSP article originally directed the State to provide free and compulsory education for children up to the age of 14?
questions.explanation: Article 45 (pre-86th Amendment) required the State to endeavor to provide free and compulsory education for all children until age 14.

questions.question: Which DPSP aims to secure a living wage, decent standard of life, and leisure for workers?
questions.explanation: Article 43 directs the State to ensure a living wage, good working conditions, and social and cultural opportunities for workers.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles is based on the Gandhian principle of village self-government?
questions.explanation: Article 40 directs the State to organise village panchayats as units of self-government, reflecting Gandhian ideals.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the promotion of educational and economic interests of Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and other weaker sections?
questions.explanation: Article 46 mandates the State to promote with special care the educational and economic interests of weaker sections, especially SCs and STs.

questions.question: The Directive Principles are described in which part of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles of State Policy are contained in Part IV of the Constitution, covering Articles 36 to 51.

questions.question: Which landmark Supreme Court case established that Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles are complementary?
questions.explanation: In the Minerva Mills case (1980), the Supreme Court held that the harmony between Fundamental Rights and DPSP is a basic feature of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle aims to secure a uniform civil code throughout the territory of India?
questions.explanation: Article 44 directs the State to endeavour to secure a Uniform Civil Code for all citizens.

questions.question: According to the Constitution, which body is responsible for the enforcement of Directive Principles?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles are non-justiciable and enforceable only by the executive and legislature, not by courts.

questions.question: Which amendment to the Indian Constitution added Fundamental Duties, complementing the Directive Principles?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added Part IV-A containing Fundamental Duties, which are linked to the spirit of DPSP.

questions.question: In the context of Tamil Nadu, which Directive Principle is most directly reflected in the state's policy of providing free breakfast in government schools?
options: A. Article 41 (right to work and public assistance)
B. Article 47 (duty to raise nutrition levels)
D. Article 45 (early childhood care and education)
questions.explanation: Article 47 directs the State to raise the level of nutrition and standard of living, which aligns with Tamil Nadu's free breakfast scheme.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles is based on the principle of 'equal pay for equal work'?
questions.explanation: Article 39(d) directs the State to ensure equal pay for equal work for both men and women.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy in the Indian Constitution are borrowed from which country's constitution?
questions.explanation: The concept of Directive Principles was borrowed from the Irish Constitution of 1937.

questions.question: Which amendment to the Indian Constitution added the words 'socialist' and 'secular' to the Preamble, impacting the interpretation of DPSPs?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added 'socialist' and 'secular' to the Preamble, reinforcing the social justice goals of DPSPs.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu government's policy, which Directive Principle is directly implemented through the 'Samathuvapuram' (Equality Village) scheme?
options: Article 46 - promotion of educational interests of SC/ST
questions.explanation: Samathuvapuram aims to create social harmony and minimize inequalities, reflecting Article 38.

questions.question: The Directive Principle under Article 48 (Organisation of agriculture and animal husbandry) is often cited to support which Tamil Nadu state policy?
questions.explanation: Article 48 directs the State to prohibit slaughter of cows and other milch animals, influencing Tamil Nadu's laws.

questions.question: In the context of Panchayati Raj, which Directive Principle is given concrete shape by the 73rd Constitutional Amendment?
questions.explanation: Article 40 directs the State to organize village panchayats, which was operationalized through the 73rd Amendment.

questions.question: The Directive Principle under Article 39A (Equal justice and free legal aid) is most directly related to which aspect of the Indian judiciary?
questions.explanation: Article 39A promotes equal justice and free legal aid, which supports the growth of PIL to access justice for the poor.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution directs the State to secure a Uniform Civil Code for the citizens?
questions.explanation: Article 44 under the Directive Principles of State Policy (Part IV) directs the State to endeavour to secure a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India.

questions.question: The 42nd Amendment Act, 1976 added which of the following Directive Principles?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment added Articles 39A, 43A, and 48A to the Directive Principles, among others.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution declares that the Directive Principles are not enforceable by any court?
questions.explanation: Article 37 states that the provisions contained in Part IV (DPSP) shall not be enforceable by any court, but they are fundamental in the governance of the country.

questions.question: Which landmark case held that Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles are complementary and should be harmoniously construed?
options: Champakam Dorairajan v. State of Madras
Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala
Minerva Mills v. Union of India
questions.explanation: In Minerva Mills (1980), the Supreme Court held that Fundamental Rights and DPSP are complementary and neither can be given primacy over the other.

questions.question: Article 39A of the Indian Constitution deals with:
options: Equal justice and free legal aid
questions.explanation: Article 39A directs the State to ensure equal justice and provide free legal aid to the poor, added by the 42nd Amendment.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle directs the State to separate the judiciary from the executive in the public services of the State?
questions.explanation: Article 50 directs the State to take steps to separate the judiciary from the executive, a liberal-intellectual principle.

questions.question: Article 48 of the Indian Constitution directs the State to:
options: Organise agriculture and animal husbandry on modern lines
Prohibit slaughter of cows and calves
questions.explanation: Article 48 directs the State to organise agriculture and animal husbandry on modern and scientific lines, and in particular to prohibit cow slaughter.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle was added by the 42nd Amendment to protect and improve the environment?
questions.explanation: Article 48A, added by the 42nd Amendment, directs the State to protect and improve the environment and safeguard forests and wildlife.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles is classified as a Gandhian principle?
questions.explanation: Gandhian principles include promotion of village panchayats, prohibition, and cow protection. Organisation of village panchayats is enshrined in Article 40.

questions.question: Directive Principles of State Policy are contained in which articles of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part IV of the Constitution, covering Articles 36 to 51, lays down the Directive Principles of State Policy.

questions.question: The Directive Principle of 'equal pay for equal work for both men and women' is mentioned in which article?
questions.explanation: Article 39(d) directs the State to ensure equal pay for equal work irrespective of gender.

questions.question: Directive Principles of State Policy are:
options: Enforceable only by the Supreme Court
questions.explanation: DPSP are not enforceable by any court but are considered fundamental in the governance of the country (Article 37).

questions.question: The Directive Principle on free legal aid is contained in which article?
questions.explanation: Article 39A, added by the 42nd Amendment, directs the State to provide free legal aid to ensure justice.

questions.question: Which Supreme Court case held that Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles are complementary and should be balanced?
questions.explanation: In Minerva Mills (1980), the Supreme Court stated that Fundamental Rights and DPSP are complementary and have a harmonious relationship.

questions.question: The Directive Principle promoting international peace and security is found in which article?
questions.explanation: Article 51 directs the State to foster respect for international law and promote peace among nations.

questions.question: Which article of the Directive Principles deals with the Uniform Civil Code?
questions.explanation: Article 44 directs the State to secure a Uniform Civil Code for all citizens throughout India.

questions.question: The concept of a 'welfare state' in the Indian Constitution is primarily embodied in:
questions.explanation: Directive Principles aim to establish social and economic justice, embodying the welfare state ideal.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are borrowed from which country's constitution?
questions.explanation: DPSP are inspired by the Irish Constitution, which itself borrowed from the Spanish Constitution.

questions.question: Which amendment added Article 39A (Equal justice and free legal aid) to the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment Act, 1976, added Article 39A to the DPSP.

questions.question: The directive to raise the level of nutrition and standard of living is mentioned in which article?
questions.explanation: Article 47 directs the State to raise the level of nutrition and standard of living and improve public health.

questions.question: In which landmark case did the Supreme Court hold that Directive Principles cannot override Fundamental Rights but should be reconciled?
questions.explanation: In State of Madras vs. Champakam Dorairajan (1951), SC held that FRs override DPSP.

questions.question: Which article of the DPSP mentions the protection of monuments and places of national importance?
questions.explanation: Article 49 directs the State to protect every monument of national importance.

questions.question: The concept of 'welfare state' in India is reflected in which part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: DPSP embody the concept of a welfare state by directing the government to promote social and economic justice.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle aims at securing the participation of workers in the management of industries?
questions.explanation: Article 43A (added by 42nd Amendment) provides for participation of workers in management.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Directive Principle under the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Right to vote is a constitutional right under Article 326, not a DPSP.

questions.question: In which part of the Indian Constitution are the Directive Principles of State Policy enshrined?
questions.explanation: Part IV (Articles 36-51) contains the Directive Principles of State Policy.

questions.question: Which amendment to the Indian Constitution added Article 43A (participation of workers in management) to the DPSP?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) inserted Article 43A among other changes to the DPSP.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle is based on the principle of 'equal pay for equal work' for both men and women?
questions.explanation: Article 39(d) directs the state to ensure equal pay for equal work for both men and women.

questions.question: Which article of the DPSP directs the state to secure a uniform civil code for all citizens?
questions.explanation: Article 44 of the Constitution directs the state to endeavour to secure a Uniform Civil Code.

questions.question: Which article declares that the Directive Principles are 'fundamental in the governance of the country' but not enforceable by any court?
questions.explanation: Article 37 states that the DPSP are fundamental in governance yet non-justiciable.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle directs the state to raise the level of nutrition and the standard of living and to improve public health?
questions.explanation: Article 47 imposes a duty on the state to raise the level of nutrition and standard of living.

questions.question: Which article of the DPSP promotes the organisation of village panchayats as units of self-government?
questions.explanation: Article 40 directs the state to organise village panchayats and endow them with necessary powers.

questions.question: In which landmark case did the Supreme Court hold that Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles are complementary to each other?
questions.explanation: In Kesavananda Bharati (1973), the Supreme Court ruled that FR and DPSP are complementary and neither is superior.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles is based on the principle of 'equal pay for equal work' for both men and women?
questions.explanation: Article 39(d) directs the state to ensure equal pay for equal work for both men and women.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are derived from the Constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: The concept of DPSP was borrowed from the Irish Constitution.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the promotion of international peace and security?
questions.explanation: Article 51 directs the State to promote international peace and security.

questions.question: In which part of the Indian Constitution are the Directive Principles of State Policy enumerated?
questions.explanation: DPSP are contained in Part IV of the Constitution (Articles 36-51).

questions.question: Which Constitutional Amendment Act added Article 43A (participation of workers in management) to the DPSP?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment Act of 1976 added Article 43A, among others.

questions.question: The Directive Principles are described as the 'conscience of the Constitution' by which scholar?
questions.explanation: Granville Austin called DPSP the 'conscience of the Constitution'.

questions.question: Which of the following cases established the harmony and balance between Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles?
questions.explanation: In Minerva Mills case (1980), the Supreme Court held that Fundamental Rights and DPSP are complementary.

questions.question: Article 45 of the Constitution originally provided for free and compulsory education for children until what age?
questions.explanation: Original Article 45 directed the state to provide free and compulsory education for all children up to age 14.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle aims to secure a uniform civil code for all citizens?
questions.explanation: Article 44 directs the state to endeavor to secure a uniform civil code.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles of State Policy is based on the principles of Gandhian ideology?
questions.explanation: Promotion of cottage industries is a Gandhian principle under Article 43, aiming to decentralize economic power.

questions.question: The DPSP are described as 'conscience of the Constitution' by which constitutional expert?
questions.explanation: Granville Austin, a renowned constitutional historian, called the DPSP the 'conscience of the Constitution'.

questions.question: Which amendment to the Indian Constitution added Article 39A to the Directive Principles?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) inserted Article 39A, directing the State to provide free legal aid to the poor.

questions.question: Which of the following cases established that Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles are complementary?
questions.explanation: In Minerva Mills (1980), the Supreme Court held that Fundamental Rights and DPSP are complementary and neither can be absolute.

questions.question: Under which Article of the DPSP does the State have a duty to raise the level of nutrition and improve public health?
questions.explanation: Article 47 directs the State to raise the level of nutrition, improve public health, and prohibit intoxicating drinks.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle was added by the 42nd Amendment to protect the environment?
questions.explanation: Article 48A, added by the 42nd Amendment, directs the State to protect and improve the environment and safeguard forests and wildlife.

questions.question: Which of the following is a socialistic Directive Principle under the Indian Constitution?
options: Equal justice and free legal aid
questions.explanation: Equal justice and free legal aid (Article 39A) is a socialistic principle aimed at reducing economic inequality.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution defines the term 'Directive Principles of State Policy'?
questions.explanation: Article 36 defines 'State' for the purposes of Part IV; Article 37 states their non-justiciable nature, but the definition is in Article 36.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy in the Indian Constitution are inspired by the Constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: The concept of DPSP was borrowed from the Irish Constitution, which itself was influenced by the Spanish Constitution.

questions.question: The Directive Principle 'equal pay for equal work for both men and women' is found under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 39(d) specifically directs the State to ensure equal pay for equal work irrespective of gender.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution directs the State to endeavour to secure a Uniform Civil Code?
questions.explanation: Article 44 (a DPSP) states that the State shall endeavour to secure a uniform civil code throughout the territory of India.

questions.question: Which Article declares that the Directive Principles are fundamental in the governance of the country and it shall be the duty of the State to apply them?
questions.explanation: Article 37 makes DPSP non-justiciable but declares them fundamental in governance, binding the State to apply them in making laws.

questions.question: By which Constitutional Amendment were the Directive Principles for free legal aid and protection of environment added?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added several DPSP, including Article 39A (free legal aid) and Article 48A (environment protection).

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles is considered a Gandhian principle?
questions.explanation: Article 48 (prohibition of cow slaughter) is a Gandhian DPSP, along with promotion of cottage industries and village panchayats.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle was added by the 86th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2002?
questions.explanation: The 86th Amendment (2002) substituted Article 45 with a new DPSP focusing on early childhood care and education for children below 6 years.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the nature of the Directive Principles of State Policy?
options: Only applicable to the Union Government
questions.explanation: Article 37 states that DPSP are not enforceable by any court but are fundamental in the governance of the country.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment added Article 48A for protection of the environment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment Act, 1976 added Article 48A for protection and improvement of the environment.

questions.question: In which case did the Supreme Court hold that Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles are complementary to each other?
questions.explanation: In Minerva Mills v. Union of India (1980), the Supreme Court held that Fundamental Rights and DPSP are complementary.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution directs the state to organize village panchayats?
questions.explanation: Article 40 directs the state to organize village panchayats and endow them with necessary powers.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution directs the state to promote international peace and security?
questions.explanation: Article 51 directs the state to promote international peace and security and maintain just and honourable relations.

questions.question: The concept of a 'Welfare State' in the Indian Constitution is primarily embodied in which part?
questions.explanation: The concept of Welfare State is embodied in Part IV (Directive Principles of State Policy) of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: Article 39A of the Indian Constitution deals with which of the following?
questions.explanation: Article 39A deals with equal justice and free legal aid to ensure that no citizen is denied justice due to economic or other disabilities.

questions.question: Which article directs the state to raise the level of nutrition and standard of living?
questions.explanation: Article 47 directs the state to raise the level of nutrition and standard of living and to improve public health.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy in the Constitution of India are borrowed from the Constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: The concept of DPSP is borrowed from the Irish Constitution, which itself was influenced by the Spanish Constitution.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: DPSPs are non-justiciable, meaning they cannot be enforced by any court, but are fundamental in the governance of the country.

questions.question: Which amendment to the Indian Constitution added the words 'socialist' and 'secular' to the Preamble, thereby influencing the interpretation of DPSP?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added 'socialist', 'secular', and 'integrity' to the Preamble, aligning with the socialist goals of DPSP.

questions.question: Under which Directive Principle does the State aim to secure a uniform civil code for all citizens?
questions.explanation: Article 44 of the DPSP directs the State to endeavour to secure a uniform civil code throughout the territory of India.

questions.question: Which of the following judgments established that Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles are complementary and should be harmoniously interpreted?
questions.explanation: In the Kesavananda Bharati case (1973), the Supreme Court held that Fundamental Rights and DPSPs are complementary and both are basic features.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution directs the State to promote cottage industries in rural areas, a principle later implemented through Panchayati Raj?
questions.explanation: Article 43 (DPSP) directs the State to promote cottage industries; Article 40 was for Panchayati Raj, but Article 43 directly relates to village industries.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu government's policy of free bus passes for all women and free breakfast in government schools reflects which Directive Principle?
options: A. Article 39(a) - Right to adequate livelihood
B. Article 41 - Right to work and public assistance
C. Article 45 - Free and compulsory education
D. Article 47 - Duty to raise nutrition levels
questions.explanation: Article 47 directs the State to raise the level of nutrition and standard of living, which is the basis for free breakfast and meal schemes.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles is based on the principle of 'equal pay for equal work' and is directly derived from the socialist ideology of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 39(d) directs the state to ensure equal pay for equal work for both men and women, reflecting socialist principles.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: (1) Directive Principles are enforceable by law. (2) Fundamental Rights are justiciable. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Fundamental Rights are justiciable (enforceable by courts), while Directive Principles are non-justiciable guidelines.

questions.question: The 42nd Amendment Act, 1976 added which of the following Directive Principles to the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 48A, added by the 42nd Amendment, directs the state to protect and improve the environment.

questions.question: In the context of the conflict between Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles, which Supreme Court case established that Directive Principles can be used to uphold the validity of a law that restricts Fundamental Rights?
options: State of Madras v. Champakam Dorairajan
Unni Krishnan v. State of A.P.
questions.explanation: In Champakam Dorairajan case (1951), the court held that Fundamental Rights prevail, but later amendments and cases like Minerva Mills balanced both.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle is specifically related to the promotion of international peace and security, and was inspired by the Atlantic Charter?
questions.explanation: Article 51 directs the state to promote international peace and security, and maintain just and honourable relations between nations.

questions.question: The Directive Principle under Article 45 originally mandated free and compulsory education for all children until they attain the age of 14 years. This was later modified by which constitutional amendment?
questions.explanation: The 86th Amendment (2002) made Article 21A a Fundamental Right and changed Article 45 to focus on early childhood care.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles is classified under the Gandhian principles and aims at promoting cottage industries on a cooperative basis?
questions.explanation: Article 43 directs the state to promote cottage industries, reflecting Gandhian economic philosophy.

questions.question: The concept of 'Welfare State' in the Indian Constitution is most closely reflected in which part?
questions.explanation: Part IV (Directive Principles) embodies the welfare state ideal by directing the state to secure social and economic justice.

questions.question: Which article of the Directive Principles directs the state to secure the right to adequate means of livelihood for all citizens?
questions.explanation: Article 39(a) directs the state to ensure that citizens have the right to an adequate means of livelihood.

questions.question: In the context of Tamil Nadu, which Directive Principle has been most actively implemented through state policies such as the 'Tamil Nadu PDS' and 'Canteen' schemes?
options: Article 43A - participation of workers in management
questions.explanation: Article 47 directs the state to raise the level of nutrition and public health, which aligns with Tamil Nadu's food security schemes.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution explicitly states that the Directive Principles of State Policy are not enforceable by any court, yet are fundamental in the governance of the country?
questions.explanation: Article 37 declares DPSP fundamental in governance but non-justiciable.

questions.question: In which landmark case did the Supreme Court hold that Directive Principles must conform to and run as subsidiary to Fundamental Rights, rejecting the view that DPSP can override FR?
options: Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala
Minerva Mills v. Union of India
S.R. Bommai v. Union of India
questions.explanation: Minerva Mills (1980) upheld the harmony between FR and DPSP, with FR having primacy.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle was inserted by the 42nd Amendment Act, 1976, and aims to secure the participation of workers in the management of industries?
questions.explanation: Article 43A was added by the 42nd Amendment for workers' participation in management.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: (i) The State can make laws abridging Fundamental Rights to implement Directive Principles. (ii) The Supreme Court can strike down a law that violates Fundamental Rights even if it seeks to implement a DPSP. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court has held that DPSP cannot override Fundamental Rights; any law violating FR is void.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu Government's policy of prohibition (e.g., the total ban on sale of liquor in some phases) draws its constitutional inspiration from which Directive Principle?
questions.explanation: Article 47 directs the State to prohibit intoxicating drinks and drugs harmful to health.

questions.question: Which article of the Directive Principles directs the State to secure for all citizens a uniform civil code throughout the territory of India?
questions.explanation: Article 44 (Uniform Civil Code) is a DPSP.

questions.question: The concept of Directive Principles of State Policy in the Indian Constitution was borrowed from the constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: DPSP were inspired by the Irish Constitution (1937).

questions.question: Which set of Directive Principles is considered 'socialistic' in nature, aiming at equitable distribution of resources and social welfare?
questions.explanation: Article 39 contains principles like adequate livelihood, distribution of material resources, etc., reflecting socialism.

questions.question: After the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, which of the following correctly describes the status of Article 40 (organisation of village panchayats)?
options: It was replaced by Part IX of the Constitution.
It remains a DPSP but Part IX gives constitutional status to panchayats.
It became a Fundamental Right under Article 21A.
It was deleted from the Constitution.
questions.explanation: Article 40 still exists as a DPSP, while Part IX provides a separate constitutional framework for panchayats.

questions.question: The 86th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2002, restructured Article 45 of the DPSP to focus on which subject?
options: Free and compulsory education for children up to 14 years
Early childhood care and education for children below 6 years
Promotion of educational interests of weaker sections
questions.explanation: After 86th Amendment, Article 45 now directs the State to provide early childhood care and education for all children until they complete the age of six years.

questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) is correct?
options: They are fundamental to the governance of the country.
They can be amended by a simple majority of Parliament.
They are superior to Fundamental Rights.
questions.explanation: Article 37 states DPSPs are fundamental to governance, though not enforceable by courts.

questions.question: The concept of 'equal justice and free legal aid' under Article 39A of the DPSP was added by which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment added Article 39A to provide free legal aid for equal justice.

questions.question: In the context of DPSP, which Article directs the State to promote cottage industries on an individual or cooperative basis?
questions.explanation: Article 43 directs the State to promote cottage industries, especially in rural areas.

questions.question: Which of the following DPSPs is based on the Gandhian principle?
questions.explanation: Article 47 (prohibition of intoxicating drinks) reflects Gandhian ideals in DPSP.

questions.question: The DPSP under Article 48A (protection of environment) was added by which amendment?
questions.explanation: Article 48A was inserted by the 42nd Amendment to protect the environment.

questions.question: Which of the following cases established that Fundamental Rights and DPSP are complementary, not subordinate?
questions.explanation: Minerva Mills case (1980) held that FR and DPSP are complementary and balance each other.

questions.question: Article 45 of the DPSP originally dealt with which subject before the 86th Amendment?
options: Free and compulsory education for children
Promotion of educational interests of SC/ST
questions.explanation: Originally, Article 45 directed free and compulsory education for children up to 14 years.

questions.question: Which DPSP Article directs the State to secure a uniform civil code throughout India?
questions.explanation: Article 44 is a DPSP that urges the State to enact a uniform civil code.

questions.question: The DPSP under Article 39(b) and (c) (equal distribution of resources) was implemented through which major land reform law in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Land Reforms Act, 1961 aimed to distribute resources equitably, reflecting Article 39(b) and (c).

questions.question: Which of the following DPSP provisions is directly related to local self-government (Panchayati Raj)?
questions.explanation: Article 40 directs the State to organize village panchayats as units of self-government.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles is specifically derived from the socialist ideology and was added to the Constitution by the 42nd Amendment Act, 1976?
options: Equal justice and free legal aid
Participation of workers in management of industries
Protection of monuments and places of national importance
questions.explanation: Article 43A (participation of workers in management) was inserted by the 42nd Amendment, reflecting socialist thought. It aims to secure democratic participation in industrial management.

questions.question: In the context of the Directive Principles, which of the following statements correctly describes the relationship between Fundamental Rights and DPSP after the Minerva Mills case (1980)?
options: Fundamental Rights are absolute and DPSP are subordinate
DPSP are supreme and Fundamental Rights must be amended to give effect to them
Both Fundamental Rights and DPSP are complementary and must be harmoniously construed
Fundamental Rights can be suspended to implement any DPSP
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court in Minerva Mills held that the harmony between FRs and DPSP is a basic feature, and neither can be given primacy over the other. They are complementary.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles is NOT a Gandhian principle as classified in the Constitution?
options: Prohibition of intoxicating drinks and drugs
Free and compulsory education for children
questions.explanation: Free and compulsory education (Article 21A, originally a DPSP under Article 45) is now a Fundamental Right; it was a liberal principle, not Gandhian. Gandhian principles include village panchayats, cottage industries, prohibition, and cow protection.

questions.question: Under the Constitution, which Article of the Directive Principles imposes a duty on the State to raise the level of nutrition and the standard of living and to improve public health?
questions.explanation: Article 47 directs the State to regard the raising of nutrition levels, living standards, and improvement of public health as its primary duties. Article 41 deals with right to work, education, and public assistance.

questions.question: Which of the following cases established that the Directive Principles cannot override Fundamental Rights but can be used to interpret the scope of Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: In Champakam Dorairajan, the Supreme Court held that DPSP must conform to FRs and cannot override them. Later cases modified this, but that case established the subordination of DPSP to FRs.

questions.question: The Directive Principle under Article 48A, which mandates protection and improvement of the environment, was inserted by which amendment?
questions.explanation: Article 48A (environment protection) was added by the 42nd Amendment in 1976 along with the Fundamental Duty under Article 51A(g). It reflects environmental awareness.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the application of Directive Principles outside India:
1. Article 37 declares DPSP are not enforceable by any court.
2. DPSP apply only within the territory of India.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct per Article 37. Statement 2 is also correct as DPSP are meant for the governance of India and do not have extra-territorial application.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles was originally included in the Constitution but later omitted and then re-introduced as a Fundamental Right through the 86th Amendment?
options: Right to free and compulsory education for children up to 14 years
Right to equal pay for equal work for both men and women
Right to just and humane conditions of work
Right to public assistance in cases of unemployment
questions.explanation: Article 45 (now Article 21A) originally a DPSP on free education was omitted and replaced; the 86th Amendment made it a FR. The other options remain DPSP.

questions.question: Under Article 40 of the Directive Principles, the State shall take steps to organise village panchayats and endow them with such powers and authority as may be necessary to enable them to function as units of self-government. Which constitutional amendment gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions, thereby implementing this DPSP?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) added Part IX to the Constitution, giving constitutional status to Panchayats, fulfilling the directive under Article 40. The 74th Amendment deals with Municipalities.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of the Directive Principles of State Policy as per the Constitution?
options: They are fundamental in the governance of the country
They can be amended by a simple majority of Parliament
They are enforceable by courts if the State fails to implement them
questions.explanation: DPSP are not enforceable by courts (Article 37). They are fundamental in governance, and can be amended by ordinary legislative process. Option D is incorrect.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment inserted Article 39A into the Directive Principles, providing for free legal aid?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment Act of 1976 added Article 39A to promote equal justice and free legal aid.

questions.question: In which landmark case did the Supreme Court first hold that Fundamental Rights are paramount over Directive Principles?
options: Champakam Dorairajan v. State of Madras
Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala
Minerva Mills v. Union of India
questions.explanation: In Champakam Dorairajan (1951), the Supreme Court ruled that Fundamental Rights prevail over Directive Principles in case of conflict.

questions.question: Which Article of the Directive Principles urges the State to promote international peace and security?
questions.explanation: Article 51 directs the State to foster respect for international law and treaty obligations, promoting peace.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu government's 'Nutritious Noon Meal Programme' primarily implements which Directive Principle?
questions.explanation: Article 47, a Directive Principle, requires the State to raise the level of nutrition and public health, directly inspiring the noon meal scheme.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle served as the constitutional basis for the 73rd Amendment Act (Panchayati Raj)?
questions.explanation: Article 40 directs the State to organise village panchayats, forming the foundation for the 73rd Amendment.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of the Directive Principles of State Policy?
options: They aim at establishing a welfare state
questions.explanation: Directive Principles are explicitly non-justiciable, meaning they cannot be enforced by courts.

questions.question: Which amendment introduced Article 48A (protection of environment) into the Directive Principles?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added Article 48A to protect and improve the environment.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu government's 'Kalaignar's Health Insurance Scheme' is inspired by which Directive Principle?
questions.explanation: Article 41 directs the State to make effective provision for public assistance in cases of sickness, underpinning health insurance schemes.

questions.question: In Minerva Mills v. Union of India (1980), the Supreme Court held that which element is part of the basic structure of the Constitution?
options: Harmony between Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles
questions.explanation: Minerva Mills ruled that the balance and harmony between Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles is a basic feature.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle was added by the 86th Amendment Act, 2002, relating to early childhood care and education?
questions.explanation: The 86th Amendment substituted Article 45 to provide for early childhood care and education for all children until age six.

questions.question: In the context of the Directive Principles of State Policy, which amendment added Article 43A to the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment Act of 1976 added Article 43A, which deals with the participation of workers in management of industries.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the relationship between Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles: 1. The Golaknath case (1967) held that Fundamental Rights cannot be amended. 2. The Kesavananda Bharati case (1973) established the basic structure doctrine. Which case first held that Directive Principles can override Fundamental Rights to implement social welfare laws?
questions.explanation: In the Quareshi case (1958), the Supreme Court held that Directive Principles can override Fundamental Rights to implement social welfare legislation.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution directs the State to promote cottage industries on an individual or co-operative basis in rural areas?
questions.explanation: Article 43 directs the State to promote cottage industries in rural areas as a Directive Principle.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are described as 'fundamental in the governance of the country' under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 37 declares that the Directive Principles are fundamental in the governance of the country, though not enforceable by courts.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle was added by the 42nd Amendment to protect the environment and wildlife?
questions.explanation: Article 48A, added by the 42nd Amendment, directs the State to protect and improve the environment and safeguard forests and wildlife.

questions.question: The concept of 'separation of judiciary from executive' in the Directive Principles is enshrined in which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 50 directs the State to separate the judiciary from the executive in the public services of the State.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles is Gandhian in nature?
questions.explanation: Article 47, which directs prohibition of intoxicating drinks, is considered Gandhian as it reflects his emphasis on temperance.

questions.question: In the Minerva Mills case (1980), the Supreme Court declared which of the following as unconstitutional?
options: The 42nd Amendment's clause giving primacy to Directive Principles over Fundamental Rights
The 44th Amendment's deletion of the right to property
questions.explanation: The Minerva Mills case struck down the 42nd Amendment's provision that gave Directive Principles primacy over Fundamental Rights, upholding the basic structure.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle is specifically linked to the Tamil Nadu government's policy of providing free electricity to farmers?
options: Article 39(b) - Distribution of material resources for common good
Article 47 - Raising level of nutrition and standard of living
Article 48 - Organization of agriculture and animal husbandry
Article 43 - Living wage and decent standard of life
questions.explanation: Article 47 directs the State to raise the level of nutrition and standard of living, which aligns with Tamil Nadu's free electricity policy for farmers.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) is correct?
options: They are enforceable by law in any court.
They are fundamental to the governance of the country.
They can be amended only by a special majority of Parliament.
They are superior to Fundamental Rights.
